reasoning: Beto O'Rourke is visibly testing the waters for a 2026 Senate run, holding town halls in Texas (e.g., Denton in April 2025, Dallas on May 19, 2025) and explicitly stating he "will" run if Texans want him to. However, the resolution criteria require him to announce before July 1, 2025, which gives a narrow ~5-week window from the May 22 knowledge cutoff. While a June announcement is plausible to capture momentum or an end-of-quarter FEC boost, candidates often extend "exploratory" phases through the summer or wait until the fall (as Beto did when he launched his 2022 Gubernatorial bid in November 2021). Furthermore, he can legally continue touring and mobilizing via his "Powered by People" PAC without formally launching a Senate committee. Given the tight time constraint and the possibility that he delays or ultimately decides against a fourth consecutive major run, the probability of a formal announcement *before* July 1 is estimated at roughly 1 in 3.
